description:
A configuration manager wants a quick per-user summary of how many configuration changes were performed recently.

The log to analyse is already on disk:

• Path: /home/user/config_logs/changes.log  
• Format (exactly one entry per line):  
  [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] user=<username> file=<absolute_path> action=<action>

Goal for the agent
1. Read /home/user/config_logs/changes.log.  
2. Count how many lines belong to each distinct <username>.  
3. Create a new file /home/user/config_logs/user_change_count.log containing exactly one line per user, in plain text, **sorted alphabetically by the username**.

Required output format of /home/user/config_logs/user_change_count.log (strictly):

username: <integer>

For example (illustrative only):

alice: 4  
bob: 2  
carol: 1

No extra spaces before or after the colon, one trailing newline at the end of the file, and no blank lines.

4. Display the contents of the newly created /home/user/config_logs/user_change_count.log to the terminal so the manager can immediately see the summary.

The entire task can be accomplished with a very small series of standard shell commands (think “2-3 commands” total). Avoid using sudo; everything is under /home/user.
truth:
Pre-existing assets before the agent starts:

Directory:
/home/user/config_logs    (exists, owned by user, 0755)

File:
/home/user/config_logs/changes.log  (0644)

Contents of /home/user/config_logs/changes.log:
[2023-07-18 09:15:23] user=alice file=/etc/nginx/nginx.conf action=modified
[2023-07-18 10:41:02] user=bob file=/etc/ssh/sshd_config action=modified
[2023-07-19 08:20:17] user=alice file=/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default action=created
[2023-07-19 11:05:44] user=carol file=/etc/cron.d/backup action=deleted
[2023-07-20 14:12:31] user=bob file=/etc/hosts action=modified
[2023-07-21 07:55:09] user=alice file=/etc/nginx/nginx.conf action=modified
[2023-07-21 12:00:00] user=carol file=/etc/passwd action=modified
[2023-07-22 09:30:45] user=alice file=/etc/nginx/sites-available/site1 action=created

Expected artefact created by agent:

File:
/home/user/config_logs/user_change_count.log  (0644)

Expected exact contents (with a single trailing newline at the very end, shown here as ↵):
alice: 4↵
bob: 2↵
carol: 2↵
name: task_000000_ad4408e7
